title: podman top: implement ps args

comments.body: I am planning to write a golang-lib to parse procFS, so we don't depend on `ps (1)` anymore. Other projects like Moby but also runc would benefit from it as well.
Agree with a manpage update being a good idea. Probably should have asked for that when I requested that we disable that arg. Oops.

This is only tangentially related but...the way pids are enumerated/managed looks racy, part of it is the cgroup API's fault.  Basically due to possible pid reuse, what we read from `/sys/fs/cgroup/pids/.../tasks` may be out of date by the time we go to invoke `ps` or read from `/proc`.  The way polkit handles this is to use the pair (pid, start-time) as a unique process identifier.  See [the code](https://cgit.freedesktop.org/polkit/tree/src/polkit/polkitunixprocess.c?id=373705b35e7f6c7dc83de5e0a3ce11ecd15d0409#n56).

I think to make this saner a kernel patch to add `/sys/fs/cgroup/pids/.../tasks-and-starttime` or so would help.  Or perhaps in the meantime, go back and verify the pid is still in the cgroup after gathering the data from `/proc`.

(Though I didn't actually verify this race exists, just was reading the code)

> I am planning to write a golang-lib to parse procFS, so we don't depend on ps (1) anymore. Other projects like Moby but also runc would benefit from it as well.

I started working on this yesterday

Here's a first version of the library: https://github.com/vrothberg/psgo

I am happy about any feedback and hope we can get it eventually under some project's umbrella to avoid me being a bottleneck. The idea of the library is to parse the ps (1) AIX format strings (e.g., "user, %C, pid, tty") and return easy-to-use tab-separated strings.
Lets get this library under github.com/containers/psgo and then we can work on it there.
Library is now in containers/psgo, @vrothberg Could you work on this issue?

> Library is now in containers/psgo, @vrothberg Could you work on this issue?

@rhatdan, with pleasure. Let's figure out some remaining issues of containers/psgo first and then I can add that to libpod.
This issue has been fixed with commit ba1871dac033783ab0329c9b3c9113a34a90992f.
